key_insight: The paper turns discrete diffusion model fine-tuning into a continuous-time reinforcement learning problem on controlled Markov chains, enabling reward-driven optimization across the whole denoising trajectory rather than only at the final step.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical training framework for improving discrete generative models such as masked diffusion LLMs, with lower-variance policy optimization and more flexible reward shaping for math and coding tasks.
method: Model RL in continuous time with a controlled continuous-time Markov chain over discrete states.
Derive continuous-time policy-gradient updates and adapt them to PPO and GRPO-style optimization.
Use trajectory subsampling to approximate expensive likelihood ratios in masked diffusion LLMs.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on low-dimensional entropy-regularized optimization problems and RL post-training of discrete diffusion LLMs for mathematical reasoning and coding tasks.
watch_item: Trajectory likelihood and per-position ratio estimation can be computationally expensive, so the subsampling approximation must be chosen carefully to avoid unstable or biased updates.
paper_title: A Continuous-Time Reinforcement Learning Framework for Fine-Tuning Discrete Diffusion Models
